name: set_read_cursor_in_a_conversation
description: Marks a message, specified by its timestamp (`ts`), as the most recently read for the authenticated user in the given `channel`, provided the user is a member of the channel and the message exists within it.

ts.description:
The timestamp of the message to mark as the most recently read. This is typically a Unix timestamp with microsecond precision (e.g., '1625800000.000200').
Examples:
  "1678886400.000100"
  "1702982400.123456"

channel.description:
The ID of the public channel, private channel, or direct message to set the read cursor for.
Examples:
  "C012QRSTUW9"
  "G012ABCDEFG"
  "D012HIJKLMN"
